phrases.intent_en: Ask where the terrace seating is
phrases.cultural_note: Terraço da Rua is the café's outdoor terrace facing the street.

likely_response.local: Siga as placas para o Terraço da Rua.
likely_response.pronunciation: SEE-guh UHSH PLAH-kuhsh PUH-ruh AW teh-RAH-soo DUH ROOUH.
likely_response.translation_en: Follow the signs to the Terraço da Rua.

responses.english: Follow the signs to the Terraço da Rua.
responses.phonetic: SEE-guh UHSH PLAH-kuhsh PUH-ruh AW teh-RAH-soo DUH ROOUH.
responses.french: Siga as placas para o Terraço da Rua.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to pick up orders
phrases.text_local: Olá, onde é que levanto a minha encomenda?
phrases.pronunciation: aw-LAH, OHNG-dih EH KIH leh-VAHNNG-too AH MEE-nyuh ayng-koo-MAYNG-duh?
phrases.cultural_note: In Portuguese cafés, 'balcão de levanta' is a common term for the counter where you collect takeaway orders.

likely_response.local: No balcão de levanta, à direita.
likely_response.pronunciation: NOO bahl-KOWN DIH leh-VAHNNG-tuh, AH dee-RAY-tuh.
likely_response.translation_en: At the pickup counter, on the right.

responses.english: At the pickup counter, on the right.
responses.phonetic: NOO bahl-KOWN DIH leh-VAHNNG-tuh, AH dee-RAY-tuh.
responses.french: No balcão de levanta, à direita.

phrases.text_local: Onde estão os pastéis de nata?
phrases.pronunciation: OHNG-dih ihsh-TOWN OOSH puhsh-TEHEESH DIH NAH-tuh?
phrases.cultural_note: In Portuguese cafés, fresh pastries are typically kept in a central glass vitrine near the counter for easy viewing and quick service.

likely_response.translation_en: They are in the pastry display case.

responses.english: They are in the pastry display case.
